rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. KAREN FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday Feb. 1, 2012 Line 1 - Defendant's Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.as untimely per CCP § 2024.020(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@

The motion should be heard by Judge Pro Tem Jeffrey Wohl if he is available and willing to hear the motion. The parties should contact Mr. Wohl to determine his availability and willingness to hear the motion and, if he is available and willing, to set a date for the hearing and to provide him courtesy copies of all papers. rulings.Case Title: CANDIE MATTSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. There are triable issues whether Plaintiff is disabled and whether Defendant discriminated and/or retaliated against Plaintiff by placing her on leave in June 2009 (i.e. Defendant's asserted non-discrimnatory reasons were pretextual). Viewed favorably to Plaintiff, the evidence shows that (1) Dr. Mandell released Plaintiff for work in December 2008, (2) Defendant accepted that release and Plaintiff began working again, (3) six months later when Plaintiff raised concerns about the danger of the GGGP Job Training Assignment and her emotional well-being, Defendant found Dr. Mandell's December 2008 release uncertain and put Plaintiff on forced leave, and (4) Defendant delayed allowing Plaintiff to return to work. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AARON FERGUSON et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off calendar as to the first and eighth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second and fifth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the third and seventh causes of action. The first and eight causes of action are not alleged against this defendant. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege: 1) ultimate facts supporting a fiduciary relationship between them and this defendant; 2) fraud with particularity; and 3) disgorgement as a remedy but not as a cause of action. A claim for conversion does not lie on these facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AARON FERGUSON et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT W. COYLE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the third, seventh and eighth causes of action. The first cause of action is adequately pled. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege: 1) fraud with particularity; 2) disgorgement as a remedy but not as a cause of action; and 3) the elements of the alter ego doctrine and that doctrine as a basis to make this defendant liable for the conduct of his alter ego under one or more claims but not as an independent cause of action. A claim for conversion does not lie on these facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELISA R SAWYER VS. GROF TRANSPERSONAL TRAINING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Grady Jefferys, Jr.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 19, Defendant Grady Jefferys, Jr.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. Per plaintiff's request, the hearing is continued to May 16, 2012 to enable plaintiff to conduct jurisdictional discovery. Until the motion is heard, no discovery is permitted as to the moving defendant other than discovery designed to determine whether the moving defendant has sufficient contacts with California to warrant personal jurisdiction over him in this State. All such jurisdictional discovery must be completed by April 25, 2012. Supplemental opposition papers, which are limited to what was obtained through the jurisdictional discovery, must be filed by May 2, 2012. Supplemental reply papers must be filed by May 9, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for an order that plaintiff's requests for admissions be deemed admitted denied per CCP § 2033.280(c). Plaintiff's motion for sanctions against defendant granted in the amount of $900.00 to be paid within ten days of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET D. FOSTER et al VS. JOHN F. BRITTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Deft'S Responses To Pltf'S Req. For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday Feb. 1, 2012 Line 6 - PLAINTIFF MARGARET FOSTER, SHERIDA IRETON's Motion For An Order Compelling Deft'S Responses To Pltf'S Req. For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for an order to compel denied as moot. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions against defendant granted in the amount of $720.00 to be paid within ten days of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: LAN LE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The opposing party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the opposition in compliance with Local Rule 2.6B and the moving papers support the sustaining of the demurrer with leave to amend. rulings.Case Title: CORAZON ZAMBRANO et al VS. NADER HEYDAYIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANTs NADER HEYDAYIAN, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST, and HAM NOURI, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE NOURI AND HEYDAYIAN FAMILY TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied. The motion serves no useful purpose. Moreover, absent some discernable prejudice to the opposing party, and none was shown here, surplus allegations are not subject to a motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF GUAM, INC., A GUAM CORPORATION VS. EAST WEST BANK, INC., A FEDERALLY CHARTERED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 1, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. As to the second cause of action, Defendant raises factual issues of contract interpretation that are not amenable to resolution by demurrer. As to the third cause of action, it is not permissible to allege a tort claim that is merely a restated claim for breach of contractual obligations. (Aas v. Superior Court (2000) 24 Cal.4th 627, 643). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
